schema_note: Each exercise has backward-compatible 'text' field plus structured 'components' with full/lean variants for the exercise component assembly system.

exercises.name: Foundation Scan
exercises.text: Starting at the top of your head, slowly bring your awareness down through your entire body. Notice your scalp, your face, your jaw. Your neck and shoulders. Your chest and belly. Your arms and hands. Your hips and legs. Your feet. Don't try to change anything. Just notice. This is your body in this moment. Say hello to it.

bridge.full: Try this before you move on. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.
bridge.lean: Try this before you move on.

intro.full: This is Foundation Scan. Attention is the first step. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.
intro.lean: This is Foundation Scan. Attention is the first step.

description.full: Starting at the top of your head, slowly bring your awareness down through your entire body. Notice your scalp, your face, your jaw. Your neck and shoulders. Your chest and belly. Your arms and hands. Your hips and legs. Your feet. Don't try to change anything. Just notice. This is your body in this moment. Say hello to it.
description.lean: Notice your scalp, your face, your jaw. Don't try to change anything. Just notice. Say hello to it.

aha.full: Now, I want you to notice something. Notice where you held tension. Whatever came up is valid. Whatever happened — or did not happen — is exactly right.
aha.lean: Now, notice where you held tension. Whatever came up is valid.

integration.full: Now, before you move on, pick one area and notice it later. Nothing has to change from this moment forward. That is enough.
integration.lean: Before you move on, pick one area and notice it later. That is enough.

exercises.name: Grounding Roots
exercises.text: Bring all your attention to your feet. Feel them on the ground. Now imagine roots growing from the soles of your feet, deep into the earth. Strong, thick roots anchoring you. You're connected to the earth. Supported. Held. Let yourself feel that stability. No matter what's happening in your life, you can always send roots down.

bridge.full: Pause here for a moment. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Grounding Roots. Your body already knows. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.
intro.lean: This is Grounding Roots. Your body already knows.

description.lean: Bring all your attention to your feet. Feel them on the ground. Strong, thick roots anchoring you. Let yourself feel that stability.

aha.full: Now, I want you to notice something. Notice if anything released. That is useful information. Whatever happened — or did not happen — is exactly right.
aha.lean: Now, notice if anything released. That is useful information.

integration.full: Now, before you move on, one scan a day changes everything. Nothing has to change from this moment forward. Keep going.
integration.lean: Before you move on, one scan a day changes everything. Keep going.

exercises.name: Tension Release Journey
exercises.text: Scan your body for tension. Where are you gripping? Your jaw? Your shoulders? Your belly? Your hands? Don't judge it. Just notice it. Now imagine breathing into that tight place. With each exhale, imagine the tension softening just a little. You don't have to force it. Just invite it to release. Even one percent softer is progress.

bridge.full: Put the book down. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Tension Release Journey. Notice before you fix. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.
intro.lean: This is Tension Release Journey. Notice before you fix.

exercises.name: Heart Center Awareness
exercises.text: Place your hand on the center of your chest. Feel your heartbeat if you can. Notice any sensations in your chest. Openness or tightness? Warmth or coolness? Expansion or contraction? Your heart center holds so much. It carries your emotions, your connections, your care. What is it feeling right now?

bridge.full: Before you keep reading. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Heart Center Awareness. Notice before you fix. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.

exercises.name: Belly Breathing Awareness
exercises.text: Place one hand on your belly. Feel it rise with each inhale, fall with each exhale. Your belly is wise. It knows how to breathe without thinking. It holds your gut feelings, your intuition. Notice what it feels like to breathe all the way down into your belly. This is where your power lives.

bridge.full: Stop here. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Belly Breathing Awareness. Notice before you fix. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.

exercises.name: Shoulder Release
exercises.text: Notice your shoulders. Are they up by your ears? Rolled forward? Tight? Take a breath in and lift them up even more. Hold. Now exhale and let them drop completely. Do this a few times. Your shoulders carry so much weight that isn't theirs to carry. What would it feel like to let them rest?

bridge.full: This one is for your body. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.
bridge.lean: This one is for your body.

exercises.name: Jaw Softening
exercises.text: Notice your jaw. Are your teeth clenched? Is there tension there? Let your tongue rest gently in your mouth. Let a tiny space form between your upper and lower teeth. Let your jaw be heavy and soft. Your jaw holds unexpressed words, swallowed emotions. Let it soften now. Let it rest.

bridge.full: Do this one out loud. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Hands Alive
exercises.text: Bring your attention to your hands. Notice any sensation there. Tingling? Warmth? Coolness? Pulse? Now rub your palms together briskly for a few seconds, then hold them an inch apart. Feel the energy between them. Your hands are incredibly sensitive. They connect you to the world through touch. What are they feeling right now?

bridge.full: Close the book for this. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Spine Awareness
exercises.text: Bring awareness to your spine. From the base of your skull all the way down to your tailbone. Your spine literally supports you. It's your central axis. Notice its natural curves. Can you sense it lengthening slightly as you breathe? Your spine is strong. It's flexible. It holds you upright while allowing you to move. Appreciate it.

bridge.full: Just thirty seconds. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Spine Awareness. Your body holds what your mind skipped. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.

exercises.name: Feet on Earth
exercises.text: Feel your feet completely. Each toe. The ball of your foot. Your arch. Your heel. Notice all the points of contact with the ground or your shoes. Your feet carry you everywhere. They're your connection to the earth. Press them gently into the ground and feel the ground pressing back. You are supported.

bridge.full: Your body needs this one. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Temperature Awareness
exercises.text: Notice temperature in your body. Where do you feel warm? Where do you feel cool? Is there a difference between your core and your extremities? Temperature is information. It tells you about your circulation, your nervous system state, your environment. Just notice what's true right now.

bridge.full: This is the practice. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Pelvic Floor Awareness
exercises.text: Bring gentle awareness to your pelvic floor, the hammock of muscles at the base of your pelvis. This area is often unconscious, but it's your foundation. It supports your organs, connects to your core. Notice any sensation there. You can gently engage these muscles and release. This is your root. Your base.

bridge.full: Right now. Not later. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Back Body Scan
exercises.text: We often forget about our back because we can't see it. Bring awareness to the entire back of your body. Your shoulders, your back ribs, your lower back, your buttocks, the backs of your legs, your heels. This is the back of you. The part that has your back. Notice what it feels like to acknowledge this whole side of yourself.

bridge.full: Try this before you move on. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Pulse Points
exercises.text: See if you can feel your pulse anywhere in your body. Your neck, your wrists, your temples, your chest. This is your heart's rhythm, the drumbeat of your life. It's been beating since before you were born. It will beat until your last moment. Feel its steady presence. You are alive. Your body is working for you right now.

bridge.full: Pause here for a moment. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Core Connection
exercises.text: Notice your core, the center of your body. Your deep abdominals, your diaphragm, your pelvic floor, your deep back muscles. This is where your power lives. Your center of gravity. Place your hands on your belly and feel this core. It's always there, supporting you, whether you're aware of it or not. Connect to your center.

bridge.full: Put the book down. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Layers of Body
exercises.text: Notice your body in layers. First, your skin, the boundary between you and the world. Then your muscles, what allows you to move. Then deeper, your bones, your structure. Then deeper still, your organs, working quietly to keep you alive. Then the fluid moving through you, blood and lymph. You are not solid. You are layers of life. Complex. Miraculous.

bridge.full: Before you keep reading. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Fluid Movement Awareness
exercises.text: Your body is mostly water. Blood flowing through your veins. Lymph moving through your tissues. Cerebrospinal fluid bathing your brain and spine. Can you sense this fluid movement? It might be very subtle. A pulse, a flow, a tide. You are not static. You are flowing, moving, circulating. Even when still.

exercises.name: Side Body Balance
exercises.text: Notice the right side of your body. Your right arm, leg, side of torso. Now notice the left side. Is there a difference? Does one side feel more alive, more tense, more open? We're rarely perfectly balanced. Just notice what's true. Your left and right sides might have different stories, different held patterns. Acknowledge them both.

bridge.full: This one is for your body. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Bone Awareness
exercises.text: Bring awareness to your bones. Your skull. Your ribs. Your pelvis. Your long bones in your arms and legs. Your spine. Bones are alive. They're constantly rebuilding themselves. They're strong enough to support you and flexible enough to absorb impact. Feel your skeleton. This is your architecture. Your internal structure.

exercises.name: Throat and Voice
exercises.text: Notice your throat. The front of your neck. This is where your voice lives. Your expression. Are you holding tension here? Is there tightness? Your throat is the bridge between your heart and your head, between feeling and speaking. What wants to be expressed but hasn't been? Just notice. You don't have to speak it yet.

exercises.name: Eye Relaxation
exercises.text: Close your eyes or soften your gaze. Notice the muscles around your eyes. Are they gripping? Let them soften. Let your eyes rest back in their sockets. Your eyes work so hard, constantly focusing, scanning, processing. Give them a moment of rest. Let them be held gently in the darkness behind your eyelids.

bridge.full: Just thirty seconds. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Eye Relaxation. Your body already knows. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.
intro.lean: This is Eye Relaxation. Your body already knows.

exercises.name: Cellular Awareness
exercises.text: You are made of trillions of cells. Each one alive, doing its job, breathing, metabolizing, communicating. Right now, cells are dividing, healing, working. Can you sense this microscopic aliveness? It might feel like a vibration, a hum, a sense of activity beneath the surface. You are not one thing. You are a universe of cooperation.

bridge.full: Your body needs this one. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Weight and Gravity
exercises.text: Feel the weight of your body. Notice gravity pulling you down. Your head is heavy. Your arms are heavy. Your legs are heavy. Instead of resisting this heaviness, surrender to it. Let yourself be held by whatever is supporting you. Gravity isn't your enemy. It's what keeps you grounded. Let it hold you.

bridge.full: This is the practice. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Throat to Belly Connection
exercises.text: Notice the central channel of your body, from your throat down through your chest and into your belly. This is your core line. As you breathe, imagine breath moving all the way down this channel. From voice to heart to gut. These centers need to be connected. When they're separate, you're fragmented. When they're connected, you're whole.

bridge.full: Right now. Not later. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Throat to Belly Connection. Attention is the first step. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.

exercises.name: Skin Boundary
exercises.text: Notice your skin, the boundary of your body. It contains you. It protects you. It's constantly sensing your environment. Feel the sensation of air or fabric against your skin. Your skin knows where you end and the world begins. This boundary is important. It keeps you safe. It defines you as separate. Appreciate it.

bridge.full: Try this before you move on. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Breath in Ribs
exercises.text: Place your hands on the sides of your ribcage. Feel your ribs expand with each inhale, release with each exhale. Your ribs are like a flexible cage, protecting your heart and lungs while allowing them to move. Breathe into your side ribs. Feel them expand three-dimensionally. This is breath at its fullest.

bridge.full: Pause here for a moment. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Inner Smile
exercises.text: Bring a very soft, subtle smile to your face. Not a big grin, just the tiniest lift at the corners of your mouth. Notice how this tiny shift changes your whole face, even your whole mood. Now bring that smile inside. Smile at your heart. Smile at your belly. Smile at yourself from the inside. This is self-kindness.

bridge.full: Put the book down. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Legs and Support
exercises.text: Notice your legs. Your thighs, your knees, your calves, your ankles. These are the pillars that support you. They carry you through your day. They're strong even when you don't feel strong. Feel the power in your legs. The muscles, the bones, the capability. Your legs support you. They've got you.

exercises.name: Neck and Head Balance
exercises.text: Your head weighs about as much as a bowling ball. Your neck supports this weight all day. Notice your neck now. Is it tense? Is your head balanced on top of your spine, or are you pushing it forward? Imagine your head floating slightly up, lengthening your neck. Let your neck do less work. Let your head balance.

bridge.full: Stop here. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Body Gratitude
exercises.text: Take a moment to thank your body. Not for how it looks, but for what it does. It breathes for you. It heals your wounds. It digests your food. It moves you through the world. Even when you criticize it, it keeps working for you. Can you feel grateful for this body, just as it is? It's doing its best for you.

bridge.full: This one is for your body. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Shoulder Blade Awareness
exercises.text: Notice your shoulder blades, those flat bones on your upper back. Some traditions call them your wings. Can you feel them? Try gently squeezing them together, then releasing. They support your arms, your gestures, your reaching. They're part of your back body, often forgotten. Bring awareness to your shoulder blades. Feel your wings.

bridge.full: Do this one out loud. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Organs Appreciation
exercises.text: Think about your organs working right now. Your heart pumping blood. Your lungs exchanging oxygen. Your stomach digesting. Your liver filtering. Your kidneys cleaning. All of this happening without you having to think about it. Your organs are intelligent. They know their jobs. Can you feel appreciation for this invisible, constant labor happening inside you?

exercises.name: Energy Body Sense
exercises.text: Beyond your physical body, can you sense an energy body? A field of aliveness that extends slightly beyond your skin? Some call this your aura, your energy field, your presence. exercises.text: Now feel your entire body as one whole system. Not separate parts, but one integrated being. From the crown of your head to the soles of your feet.
